rulings.Case Title: VICKY WOOLLEY, et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 10, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Pacific Coast Building Products, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is continued on the court¿s own motion to March 16, 2011 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 220. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES DUNGY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 10, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Milwaukee Valve Company, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony precludes defendant from invoking the factually devoid prong discussed in Aguilar with regard to plaintiff being in the vicinity of valves attributable to defendant. of Defendant¿s separate statement failed to contain sufficient facts that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that the valves attributable to defendant that plaintiff was exposed to did not contain asbestos. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LEVY SHIELDS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 10, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Alta Building Material¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff did not present any admissible evidence that he was exposed to an asbestos-containing product supplied by defendant. Defendant¿s objections to the deposition testimony of Mr. Hamilton, Hamilton¿s interrogatory responses and the declaration of Mr. Ay are sustained. Plaintiff failed to show that defendant received notice of the 1996 deposition of Mr. Hamilton. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LARRY CAMPBELL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Familian Corporations Notice Of Motion By Familian Coproation To Dismiss For Delay In Prosecution Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 583.310 And 583.360
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 10, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Familian Corp.¿s motion to dismiss for delay in prosecution pursuant to C.C.P. §§ 583.310 & 583.360 is granted. No opposition filed. Hearing required unless counsel for defendant informs Mr. Campbell of this tentative ruling and he states that he does not wish to contest. = (220/HEK)
